teaching_language: Combined Italian and English; the first three semesters are taught in English and the following three are taught in Italian.

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need an upper secondary school diploma (or equivalent) and a minimum 12-year school record. If the school system is shorter than 12 years, applicants must add eligible extra academic study, a post-secondary qualification, or an Italian foundation course. Open-access applicants may complete the entry qualification by 31 August 2026.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: No programme-specific school-subject prerequisite was stated on the official English programme page or apply factsheet.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: No GPA requirement was stated. Applicants must verify the country-based entry-title rules, and if their home system requires a university entrance exam for higher education access, proof of passing that exam is required.

language_requirements.required_level: English B2 (CEFR). No Italian certificate is required at application stage because the official factsheet states that students will reach B2 Italian after the first three semesters.
accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training: minimum 6.0 for B2.
Trinity College London Integrated Skills in English ISE II.
Oxford Test of English: minimum 126 for B2.
GATEHOUSE ESOL International Classic B2: GA Level 1 Certificate.
Pearson Test of English General: PTE General 3.
Pearson English International Certificate: Level 3 Upper Intermediate.
Pearson Test of English Academic: minimum 65 for B2.
exemption_rules: No certificate is required if the previous education leading to the entry qualification was completed entirely in English.
No certificate is required for native English speakers from the countries listed by the University of Padua.

application_deadline_eu.notes: The third call is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The official factsheet states that the third call is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport or valid ID card
required_documents.notes: EU-equivalent applicants may use a valid ID card; other applicants normally provide a passport.
Required only for non-EU applicants already residing in Italy.
Documents must be official and written or translated in Italian or English.

required_documents.document_name: Upper secondary school certificate or academic qualification (if already obtained)
required_documents.notes: No legalised documents are required at application stage.
Listed in the general application document checklist.
Listed in the general application document checklist.
Required unless the applicant qualifies for an exemption. No Italian certificate is required at application stage.

required_documents.document_name: Legalised upper secondary diploma translated into Italian or English
required_documents.notes: Required after admission to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: Proof of passed home-country university entrance exam, if required by the education system
required_documents.notes: Required only when the country of qualification requires such an exam for university access.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment and D visa for study-enrolment
required_documents.notes: Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol on Universitaly by 15 July and then apply for a study visa.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No programme-specific entry exam stated on the official English sources [uncertain]
entry_exam_or_test.dates: No programme-specific test dates were stated on the official English programme page or apply factsheet [uncertain].
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The official English sources state open access with requirements, but they do not describe a separate entry exam, assessment test, or score threshold [uncertain].

source_quotes.quote: no Italian language certification is required

uncertainty_notes: The degree is not fully English-taught: the first three semesters are in English and the following three are in Italian.
No separate 2026/2027 admission-notice PDF was linked from the official English factsheet, so the apply.unipd.it course factsheet is used as the official call reference.
The official English sources do not clearly describe a separate assessment test despite the open-access-with-requirements wording; see entry_exam_or_test [uncertain].
